core_fields.question: When did this incident happen (date/time if known)?
What harm, loss, or impact did you suffer?
What evidence do you currently have?

fields.question: Do you have invoice/receipt or order proof?
How did the seller/manufacturer respond?
What remedy do you want: refund, replacement, repair, compensation?
Which platform/store was used for purchase?
What was your role and employment type?
Which dues are pending (salary, gratuity, notice pay)?
Do you have appointment letter/salary slips/work records?
How did the employer respond to your grievance?
Do you know the accused identity?
Have you approached police or filed any complaint/FIR?
Is there any immediate threat to your safety?
Where did the incident happen?
What is your relationship to the property (owner/tenant/landlord)?
Do you have agreement/title/lease documents?
Who currently has possession of the property?
What is the approximate value/rent amount involved?
What is the family relationship involved?
Are children involved in this dispute?
Do you need urgent interim relief?
What is the current living arrangement?
What type of contract/agreement is involved?
What exact breach occurred?
Have you sent a legal/contractual notice?
What is the approximate contract value?
Which app/platform/channel was used?
Do you have screenshots, emails, logs, or transaction IDs?
Have you reported this to cybercrime portal/police/bank?
Were any accounts/devices compromised?
What financial instrument is involved (loan, credit card, etc.)?
What amount is involved?
How did the bank/financial institution respond?
Is there any payment default history?
What type of insurance policy is involved?
Was the claim rejected, delayed, or partially settled?
Do you have written communication from the insurer?
Which authority issued the notice/order?
Do you have the notice number/reference?
Have you filed any reply/appeal so far?
What caused the injury or loss?
Who do you believe is responsible?
What medical/repair costs have you incurred?
What type of entity is involved (private/public/LLP)?
What is your role (shareholder/director/creditor)?
What commercial relief are you seeking?
What result are you seeking?
What type of injury occurred?
How severe is the injury (minor/moderate/severe)?
Do you have medical records or treatment papers?
What medical expenses have you incurred?

strategy_hints: Include compensation and liability pathways.
Ask for injury proof, treatment documents, and expense computation.

fields.question: What was the reported cause of death?
Are there dependents of the deceased?
Is a postmortem or medical report available?

strategy_hints: Prioritize urgent procedural and compensation tracks.
Capture dependent details and official reports.

fields.question: What false representation was made?
Did you transfer money/property due to this?
What amount/value was lost?
How was the transaction made (UPI/bank/card/cash)?

strategy_hints: Add criminal and recovery routes in parallel.
Prioritize transaction evidence and traceability details.

fields.question: How often has the harassment occurred?
Do you have call logs/messages/recordings/witnesses?
Where and in what context did this happen?

strategy_hints: Assess urgency and immediate protection options.
Structure evidence timeline for repeated incidents.

fields.question: Were you given written notice or termination letter?
What reason was stated for termination?
How long were you employed?

strategy_hints: Prioritize reinstatement/dues/compensation analysis.
Check notice compliance and contractual terms.

fields.question: What product/service is defective?
What is the exact defect/problem?
How was it being used when the defect appeared?

strategy_hints: Prioritize refund/replacement/repair first.
Escalate to liability only if injury or major damage is present.

fields.question: What amount remains unpaid?
When did the payment become due?
What document/arrangement created this payment obligation?

strategy_hints: Include recovery pathway and interest/compensation considerations.

fields.question: What was the promised timeline?
How much delay has occurred?
What loss did this delay cause?

strategy_hints: Include delay compensation and timeline breach analysis.

fields.question: Which key documents do you have?

strategy_hints: Leverage documentary evidence to strengthen grounds.

fields.question: How many times did you contact them, and through which channels?

strategy_hints: Include escalation after non-response and communication log use.

fields.question: How much has already been paid?
How much balance is still unpaid?

strategy_hints: Differentiate admitted liability from disputed balance.

fields.question: What terms were agreed verbally?
Were any witnesses present during this verbal agreement?

strategy_hints: Prioritize corroborative evidence and conduct-based proof.

fields.question: Which platform/payment rail was used?
Do you have transaction reference IDs?

strategy_hints: Use transaction traceability and platform complaint channels.

fields.question: What is the FIR/complaint number, if available?

strategy_hints: Align civil strategy with existing criminal procedural status.

fields.question: When was the legal notice sent?
Was there any response to the legal notice?

strategy_hints: Incorporate pre-litigation conduct into final strategy.

fields.question: What is the existing case number, if any?
What stage is the existing case currently at?

strategy_hints: Avoid conflicting remedies and account for pending proceedings.

fields.question: What kind of threat was made?
Is there immediate urgency or safety concern?

strategy_hints: Prioritize immediate protection and emergency procedural steps.

fields.question: How many previous incidents occurred and when?

strategy_hints: Build a chronology and pattern evidence for stronger relief.

fields.question: Was any notice served before the dispute escalated?
Where is the property located?

strategy_hints: Prioritize possession status, notices, and title/tenancy evidence.
